Tomoe was a woman, after all — of course she didn't hate sweets. She adored them.

Even as Tomoe screamed that in her head... her eyes remained fixated on the cake being carried to the table. ...She was starting to feel bloodlust toward the ecstatic customers sitting in front of the colorful cake.
"The main item for today is the mango tart with melon accents..."
...Is that what they call this cake? With all the fruit decorating the top, you can't even see the surface!!
"Oh, and for today only, we are also preparing strawberry tarts.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_waitFb2008. What do you think?"
"By the way, this meal is on me.@k@vS11/30/420200136. So don't hold back. Eat as much as you like."

And at last...
Tomoe finally abandoned her pride.
"...Wow, you ate a lot, Minai-kun.@k@vS11/30/420200138. So, are you satisfied?"
"N-not really...@k@vS11/28/420100262. Even after all these sweets, it doesn't really feel like I've had lunch."
With that, Tomoe turned to look away.
...However, she happily enjoyed eating sweets to her heart's content. Every time Tomoe's cheeks seemed to puff out, she poured some black coffee down her throat.
(Aah, but maybe I shouldn't have cake for a while...)
Of course, she had already ingested a ridiculous number of calories.
She considered going for a swim tomorrow at the public pool... but as she thought about that, she signaled for the waitress to pour her some more coffee.
"I've heard rumors of your eating habits, but wow.@k@vS11/30/420200140. I never expected you to eat four whole cakes."
"...Don't you dare suggest splitting the bill."
"Ha ha, a man doesn't go back on his word."
...Although, Yamaoki handled it as though he expected as much. Even after watching Tomoe's gluttony with his own eyes, he showed no signs of being upset.
(He should at least act a little surprised.)
She wasn't particularly interested in boasting about her gluttony, but it was awkward how easily he accepted it.
"Anyway... I'd expect no less from Yuusuke's daughter.@k@vS11/30/420200143. Like father, like daughter."

Tomoe's expression hardened as soon as she heard that name, and she picked up her cup. ...But the cup was still too hot, so she sat it back down on the saucer and took a drink from the cold cup next to it.
...'Yuusuke' was none other than her father's name.

"I'm grateful for all the information you've provided me, even before I started as a police officer.@k@vS11/28/420100273. Thanks to that, I've been able to learn information I never would've been able to find on my own... and I can continue the search, even now."
Her encounter with Yamaoki was one of the reasons Tomoe had aspired to become a police officer.

TextENGNew: And then, she looked up in surprise when she learned said home was actually his own destination...
"This is your house?@k@vS11/11/420300002. ...Then, are you Minai-san's daughter?"
"Yes.@k@vS11/28/420100275. ...I'm the older daughter, Tomoe."

"So, that's who you are...@k@vS11/11/420300004. Oh, please forgive me.@k@vS11/11/420300005. I only recently found out that he passed away.@k@vS11/11/420300006. ...Would it be all right for me to burn a stick of incense in his honor?"
"Oh, sure. ...Go ahead."
Then, after explaining the situation to her grandmother, Ooishi entered the house and clapped his hands together in front of the Buddhist altar in the living room.
She couldn't make out his facial expression very well from behind... but she could hear a faint sigh that indicated how he was feeling.
"...Minai-san and I worked together on a case a long time ago.@k@vS11/11/420300008. He was... always carrying a photo of his two daughters close to him. He was extremely proud of both of you."
"...I promised that we'd get drunk together after he became a big shot."
Tears came gushing out of Tomoe's eyes.
The grief she had been feeling all this time combined with Madoka's disappearance took a heavy toll on her, and it wouldn't stop.
Damn it...! She had already convinced herself she wouldn't cry in front of other people...!
"...@k@vS11/11/420300011.I don't know what happened, but please cheer up.@k@vS11/11/420300012. Otherwise, your parents won't be able to rest in peace."
"...Then please, catch the criminal who killed my mom and dad!@k@vS11/28/420100279. He ruined our lives... but he's still living a carefree life out there...!!"
Tomoe knew it was unreasonable... but she couldn't help but lash out at Ooishi, who was standing right in front of her.
However, he paid it no heed... and instead put a hand on her shoulder to comfort her.
"It's all right.@k@vS11/11/420300014. Minai-san's colleagues are desperately searching, even now.@k@vS11/11/420300015. I'm certain they'll catch the killer..."
His hand was large, and very warm. And so, tears came spilling from her eyes once again...
"Now then... Tomoe-san, was it?@k@vS11/11/420300017. The truth is, there's something I want to ask you."
"Something... to ask me?"
"That's right. About the case... your father was investigating up until he died.@k@vS11/11/420300019. I believe those people were the ones who murdered him."
After hearing Ooishi say that... Tomoe couldn't help but gasp. She was speechless.
Mom and Dad... were murdered?
It wasn't just a random crime from a reckless arsonist or vagrant... but a planned conspiracy...?!

"...It's getting late today, so I'll visit you again tomorrow.@k@vS11/11/420300021. When I do, I'll be bringing along your father's old boss.@k@vS11/11/420300022. ...So, would you be willing to tell us what you know?"
And the next day, Ooishi introduced her to Yamaoki, who was her father's boss. That just went to show how they had full confidence in her.
And the one who encouraged her to become a police officer was none other than Yamaoki. He asked if instead of relying on others, she wanted to carve out her fate with her own hands...
That's why Tomoe was able to consult with him almost as though he were a parent.
Their relationship only went sour after last year. Only after he and Madoka...
"...You have reservations about the relationship between Madoka and I, and I'm sorry for that.@k@vS11/30/420200152. I'm well aware of the age difference, of course.@k@vS11/30/420200153. However..."
"I'm not interested in receiving an apology.@k@vS11/28/420100284. If she heard about it, it would only make her depressed."
Yamaoki said only that, and understood everything.
...Madoka had already decided to marry Yamaoki. If he felt any shame about it now, it could be seen as looking down on Madoka's feelings.
No matter what she was thinking, she had already made up her mind...
"In fact, please let me apologize... for being stubborn, emotional, and for all the unspeakable things I've said to you."
"You've always been like a father to me.@k@vS11/28/420100287. So I think I didn't know how to handle it... and ended up acting like a spoiled brat."
"No, don't worry about it.@k@vS11/30/420200157. In fact, I've been all alone until recently.@k@vS11/30/420200158. These parent and child games are rather fun."
"Ahahaha..."
Tomoe smiled awkwardly... but her attention lay elsewhere.
Should she make certain of it... right here and now?
This was a great opportunity to ask Yamaoki about the rumors and his feelings for Madoka.
"Do you truly love Madoka?"
"Did Madoka ask you to do something for me?"
Yamaoki had to know about the rumors regarding him and Madoka. If she asked about it, he would probably give a straight answer.
However... she also understood that the question was too wretched and despicable to ask.
(...In the end, I can't ask.)
So, Tomoe ended up swallowing the impulse... and opted to ask him about something else.
"...Chief Yamaoki.@k@vS11/28/420100292. There's... something I need to ask you."
"There's something I've wanted to ask you whenever an opportunity came up.@k@vS11/28/420100294. ...Please be honest with me."
"What is it?"

"You came to Kakiuchi from the public safety division of the Metropolitan Police Department last year. And you invited me here this year.@k@vS11/28/420100296. ...Is it because of some progress on my father's case?"
Ever so slightly,.
Yamaoki furrowed his eyebrows... and stared straight ahead, directly at Tomoe.
However, she couldn't read anything from his facial expression.
"...Why do you think that?"

"Because otherwise, I can't think of a reason for transferring from a station in the Kanto region to the Chubu region.@k@vS11/28/420100298. And several other related parties say there aren't many precedents for that."
Executive candidate police officers are often transferred to various locations to gather experience as part of their training.
However, the fact that Tomoe was transferred from the Kanto region to the Chubu region was an exceptional case that could only be accomplished with a very strong request.
Yamaoki raised his glass of coffee to his mouth and carefully stared at Tomoe as he took a sip.
...Her eyes looked just like they did back when Ooishi first introduced him to her.
"Tomoe-kun."
Tomoe felt a little surprised by that response.
...It had been a long time since he had referred to her by her first name.
"Are you... still chasing the person who killed your parents?"
"Of course."
"So you still hold a grudge against the perpetrator...?"
Tomoe quietly bit her lip. ...There was no need to answer, her expression made it abundantly clear.
"It's been 11 years.@k@vS11/30/420200168. I think Yuusuke would be proud of all you've accomplished.@k@vS11/30/420200169. But isn't it about time to move on with your life?"
"...Right now, this is everything to me.@k@vS11/28/420100303. I can't move forward until we solve my parents' murder."
"...@k@vS11/30/420200171.Is that what you're trying to convince yourself of?"
Convinced...?
She had never thought about it in those words.
And honestly... deep down, she felt insulted.
She was following the case of her parents' murder, trying to find the person responsible...
That was the goal she set for herself, even before deciding to become a police officer.
It was Tomoe's greatest dream and aspiration, not some weak sentiment she had to "convince" herself of.
Yet, why did he say it like that...?
"...No, sorry.@k@vS11/30/420200173. I didn't mean anything weird by that... so, please don't make that face."
Tomoe quickly turned away as Yamaoki said that with a bitter smile on his face.
...What kind of face was she making?
"Unfortunately, there hasn't been any progress.@k@vS11/30/420200175. I just thought it would be better for you to carry out your investigation here."
Even though she nodded to him, she still wasn't convinced.
First off, if the intent was to make investigating her father's case easier, Tomoe could've simply been transferred to prefectural police HQ in Aichi rather than Kakiuchi Station.
Not to mention, Yamaoki had no reason to abandon his normal job and take a post in this town.
Nevertheless... this man has been hiding something, this whole time.
Tomoe still couldn't understand his intentions.
"Is that all you have to say?"
"...@k@vS11/28/420100309.I'm sorry for asking something so weird.@k@vS11/28/420100310. ...It's almost time for my train, so would you please excuse me?"
With Yamaoki's consent, Tomoe rose to her feet. And then, she walked out of the store without looking back.
"Hey, Yuusuke.@k@vS11/30/420200179. Your daughter is just like you, down to all your worst traits."
"...Haah."
As she left the store, the sun was beginning to set.
"That's Hibiya Park right there... so I should be able to walk to the next station."

This was her first time in Tokyo in a long time so the buildings looked a little different, but the Kasumigaseki district was lined with government office buildings, so she had no doubt about where she was.
Although she was feeling a little lost, turning to a police box to ask for directions would reflect poorly on her as a detective, so she resolved to reach her destination all on her own.
"...Haah."
Her head cooled off as she walked... and she started to feel enough regret to make her body feel heavy.
(...I should've asked about Madoka.)
Even though it was a golden opportunity, she didn't have the courage to step up.
"In the end, maybe I just don't believe in them.@k@vS11/28/420100315. The chief, and Madoka..."
That thought started to make her feel depressed. In that case, it would've been better to make a big scene opposing their marriage...
Just outside the station, the hustle and bustle of cars coming and going was quieter than expected... and instead, she could hear the loud sound of a microphone.
And the large number of people walking on the sidewalk stopped to turn their attention at something.
"...All eligible voters in transit, please excuse the disturbance!!"
She finally reached the front of the crowd and had a good view. ...The loud noise was coming from an election campaign car.
(Aah, come to think of it, elections are coming up at the end of the year.)
"Pardon me, I know you're all busy! I would like to borrow just a moment of everyone's precious time!!"
Naturally, people stopped walking to turn toward the source of this loud voice.

And then, after looking at the party and candidate names written on the banner on the side of the car... some resumed walking, but those who were at least a little interested continued listening attentively.
"Thank you very much for listening!"
"Now everyone, let me ask you this. Can you truly say that you're satisfied with the health care and the pharmaceutical businesses in our country?"
"Right now, various medications are currently being sold to ordinary households, but the prices are high and their efficacy is uncertain. Plus, there are risks of side effects."
"In order to solve those problems, pharmaceutical companies, medical experts, and laboratory staff have been working themselves to the bone, carrying out clinical trials day and night."
"Unfortunately, the way things are now, we'll never reach the level of knowledge and state-of-the-art technology present in Western countries."

"For that reason, many Japanese medical institutions have been relying on imported pharmaceuticals, but that approach has the disadvantage that we can't accurately grasp the potential side effects of the medicine."
"It's a very sad thing, but I'm sure everyone still remembers the disastrous side effects caused by Lowell Inc. last year..."
...The Lowell incident.

Lowell Inc. is one of the largest businesses in its home country, and regarded as one of the top three in the world among the pharmaceutical industry. Patients who took drugs manufactured by Lowell started suffering intense fevers one after another, sometimes with fatal results.

The medicine was manufactured and sold for the purpose of treating mental disorders... but even from the start, there were questions about the source ingredients, and experts were speaking out in the name of public safety.
Nevertheless, it passed through preliminary tests rather easily, and began circulation as medical product, in part due the credibility of Lowell's corporate brand...

But more than that, key personnel in the pharmaceutical safety bureau and executive management received kickbacks as a show of gratitude... The bribery came to light after a whistleblower made public accusations.
And simultaneously, they tried to cover it up... but once the series of patient deaths came to light, it became a massive scandal that destroyed the credibility of the medical world at the time.
...An unfortunate accident.
The lives lost couldn't be so easily cleaned up... but the wrongdoers still lined their pockets.
Things have stabilized by now, but the cabinet at the time completely collapsed... and the situation developed in a way that the majority of seats abandoned the major ruling party.

...After that, the review board researchers, public welfare officials, and pharmaceutical company executives involved in the incident were arrested and prosecuted. And civil lawsuits were conducted by the victims and bereaved families.
In addition to that, a former member of the public welfare office in parliament was arrested for their involvement behind the scenes, and the trial is scheduled for next year...
However, for most people it's a thing of the past. And the ruling party eliminated key personnel and rebuilt itself in order to portray themselves as an unrelated third party...
The campaign official standing atop the election car continued fervently discussing her opinions with the audience.
...No, it wasn't a discussion. It was a one-sided rant.
Because her eyes weren't facing the audience, but toward the red carpet beyond them...

"And at this time, please welcome the current Minister of Health and Welfare, Akihiko Chiba, who is supporting Minoru Yasukawa in the upcoming winter House of Councillors election!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_uguL2011. Everyone, thank you very much for listening so patiently!!"
As a thunderous applause rang out, Tomoe slowly gazed up at the white-haired man standing atop the election car holding microphones in both hands.

She had seen him on TV several times. He was the head of the major ruling party, and was originally from the Ministry of Health and Welfare. He often appeared holding a large pipe, and had a burning passion for medical reform.
And if the House of Councillors elections proceed the way they're leaning, the House of Representatives elections next year will be completely settled.
That's why a "big shot" like him was supporting this. To give himself more name recognition for his own campaign.
"...As Yasukawa has already said, there are still many problems within our medical system."
"First and foremost, illness isn't merely a physical ailment. It can also affect the mind."
"Therefore, I believe it's only natural for us to seek the means to alleviate or completely solve these problems."
"However, Japan currently lacks the necessary research facilities and know-how."
"Only after solving these problems can our nation reach a modern level comparable to Western countries. That is my dearest wish for our citizens.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_chiba2006. Also..."
"...Incomprehensible, as usual."
As she grimaced from the throbbing of her eardrums, Tomoe made her way through the intently listening crowd.
First off, it was hard to trust the word of someone talking about "our nation" and "citizens."
After all, they do whatever they want and just make it sound good to plausibly justify their actions.
Arguing it's for the sake of others is one thing, but actually doing it in practice is a big deal.
But when it doesn't go well for those people, they won't admit their mistakes. They don't have the courage to go back and start over.
It's completely selfish to run away, but isn't it odd how the people don't hold them accountable...?
"...Huh? Minai?"
Just then, a man wearing a suit and passing out flyers looked up at Tomoe with a surprised expression. And after carefully examining her face, he suddenly began to smile.
"Aah, it's really you, Minai!@k@vS11/33/420800003. It's been so long. Are you doing well?"
"Ah, yeah..."
She ambiguously nodded and embarrassedly scratched her neck while thinking, "Who is this?"
...But Tomoe caught her breath as soon as she remembered.
"...Senpai?@k@vS11/28/420100323. Tsukada-senpai?"
"Hold on, have you forgotten about me?@k@vS11/33/420800005. Now that's a little depressing."
"W-Well yeah, of course I didn't know it was you.@k@vS11/28/420100325. I could hardly recognize you."
While saying that, Tomoe took another look at the young man... Osamu Tsukada.
(Seriously, I couldn't recognize him...)
He was her senior on the high school track and field team, an ace at short distance running.
He regularly competed in national competitions, and he was popular around the school... Tomoe had vague memories of the time.
Even when she decided to quit the track team, he persuaded her to reconsider until the end. And even after she officially withdrew, they still stayed close and talked with each other.
Nonetheless, they became separated after Tomoe entered Ibaraki University, and they lost contact...
"Anyway, what are you doing now, Tsukada-senpai?"
"Me?@k@vS11/33/420800007. Fu fu, feast your ears on this.@k@vS11/33/420800008. Actually, I'm working as the secretary for a certain congressman."
"A certain... what?"
Then, Tomoe looked at the flyers in his hand. And there, she saw the face of the Minister of Health and Welfare, Chiba, who was giving that speech on the election car just a moment ago.
"Is it, perhaps, Minister Chiba?"
"Ahahaha, exactly!@k@vS11/33/420800010. I'm Chiba-sensei's second secretary.@k@vS11/33/420800011. Although, handing out flyers like this isn't what I signed up for."
"Don't say that.@k@vS11/28/420100331. That's really cool."
Tomoe responded from the bottom of her heart.
Come to think of it, Tsukada did once say he was pursuing the department of political science and economics because he wanted to become a politician.
And the college he ended up attending was a high-ranking private university that has produced a lot of congress members in the past.
But the enrollment fee was far higher than Tomoe could manage...
"You're doing your best to pursue your dreams. That's amazing.@k@vS11/28/420100333. At the rate you're going, you'll be a legislator yourself before long."

"Ahahaha, that's still a long ways out.@k@vS11/33/420800013. For now, I'm just carrying the boss's bags, passing out flyers, and studying!@k@vS11/33/420800014. ...By the way, Minai, what are you up to nowadays?"
"Huh? Me...?"
She hesitated for a moment.
After listening to that whole campaign speech, somehow she couldn't bring herself to say, "I'm a detective."
"I'm in Tokyo today for a brief business trip.@k@vS11/28/420100336. Right now, I'm working in Kakiuchi, Aichi prefecture."
"Kakiuchi?@k@vS11/33/420800016. Wow, I see.@k@vS11/33/420800017. That's close to Chiba-sensei's hometown."
"Eh...? Really?"
Come to think if it, she did seem to recall seeing him on a poster somewhere in town.
However, she didn't realize it was a congressman as important as the Minister of Health and Welfare.
"I'm scheduled to head to that area for next year's campaign, so maybe we can meet up somewhere... What do you say?@k@vS11/33/420800019. If we meet up, let's have a meal next time."
"...You're the same as ever, senpai."
"Ahahahaha, if anyone is the same as ever, it's you.@k@vS11/33/420800021. Even though you were wearing a suit and at a distance, I recognized you immediately."
Then, a voice came from a short distance away shouting, "Tsukada-saaan." Tsukada quickly responded to that call, then reached into his pocket and pulled out a business card holder.
"Sorry, I have business to attend to after this.@k@vS11/33/420800023. Oh, here's my business card.@k@vS11/33/420800024. If you'd like, feel free to contact me in your spare time."
Tsukada placed the business card into Tomoe's hand as he said that, then quickly walked off.
Then, his back was swallowed by the crowd in the blink of an eye.
"Haven't changed at all, huh...?"
That was probably just social etiquette, without any special meaning.
However,.
At this particular moment, Tomoe felt like it was admonishing her for her inability to move on from the past... and her chest began to ache.
"...Ah, Grandma?@k@vS11/28/420100343. Yeah, yeah... That's right, I'm leaving Ueno Station now.@k@vS11/28/420100344. My work is complete, so I'll be heading over shortly."
"Huh? ...Yeah, right.@k@vS11/28/420100346. I'm taking the express train, so I'll arrive around 10 o'clock."
"Ahaha, I'll be fine.@k@vS11/28/420100348. I'm not a child anymore.@k@vS11/28/420100349. I have a key, so you can head to bed early. ...Anyway, later."

After hanging up the receiver on the public phone, Tomoe grabbed the remaining 10 yen coin from the coin return and put it in her pocket.@k And just as she turned around, an announcement came out that the train was arriving at the station.
Though it was a holiday, there weren't a lot of people in the car, perhaps because of the late hour.
"...Haah."
She sat down at her designated seat by the window, and set her bag down on the seat next to her.
Then, she leaned back against the backrest to get comfortable and spread open the tabloid she bought at the station shop.
The page she opened to had a picture of Minister Chiba, who was giving that speech earlier.
A massive headline was written next to the picture, saying: "Promoting Medical Reform. Proposing Measures to Reduce Burden on Elderly."
(If he's really such a famous legislator, then maybe it won't be long at all before senpai becomes a "legislator" himself.)
But beyond that, she no longer had any feelings for Tsukada.
Seeing him for the first time in a while, and seeing he was just the same as always... reminded her of the day they broke up.
"...@k@vS11/28/420100354.Oh, it's already time for the GI race, huh?"
Intending to change the mood, Tomoe looked down on the page in front of her and saw a column about horse racing.
She wasn't very fond of gambling, but she did follow horse racing since she was young, both as a way to kill time and to have a topic to discuss with elderly people.
In fact, making carefree predictions about the outcome can kill an hour just as well as a crossword puzzle, without any boredom.
So, it was the last thing she looked forward to from the evening paper.
"Well, they've been holding it in Kyoto for a while now, so Nakayama shouldn't be very crowded.@k@vS11/28/420100356. I wonder if I can buy some tickets tomorrow. ...Huh?"
Just then.
The moment Tomoe turned the page and casually noticed a local news article... all thoughts completely vanished from Tomoe's mind.
"It can't be...?!"
What was written there... was the story of a patient who fell from a local hospital.
However, the name of the patient who died in the accident was one she would never forget... It was a boy she had interviewed before, a victim of the "schoolgirl campus assault" incident.
